channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Most of the architects of October 7 attacks have been killed, says Netanyahu
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Sunday that Israel is close to finishing a key war aim in Gaza: killing the planners of the October 7 attacks. The comments came after Israel said it had killed senior Hamas commander Ezzedine Al-Haddad in an airstrike, as its campaign against the group’s leadership continues.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russia: Zelensky says response to Moscow's agression 'justified'
item.description: Douglas Herbert has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Drone hits UAE nuclear power plant: IAEA reports normal radiation levels
item.description: Hoda Abdel-Hamid is live from Doha, Qatar.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: WHO declares emergency as Ebola outbreak kills more than 80 in DR Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization declared an international health emergency on Sunday over an outbreak of an Ebola strain in the Democratic Republic of Congo that has killed more than 80 and for which there is no vaccine.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ebola outbreak declared health emergency in DR Congo and Uganda
item.description: Ariel Kestens, Head of IFRC Kinshasa Delegation, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: "One Nation under God": US President Trump joins national prayer rally
item.description: André Gagné, Full Professor and Chair, Concordia University of Montreal, Canada, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia kills at least 4 and wound 12 others
item.description: One of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russia killed at least four people, including three near Moscow, and wounded a dozen others, local authorities said Sunday. Debris fell on Russia's largest airport without causing damage.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ebola: WHO warns about risk of spread in DR Congo and Uganda
item.description: Trish Newport, Emergency Programme Manager for MSF's Ebola response in Ituri, DRC, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Are Google and SpaceX plotting space-based data centres?
item.description: Tech giants are increasingly looking beyond Earth to solve one of AI's biggest growing pains: where to put all the computers.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Lam

item.title: UK leadership crisis: ‘Keir Starmer is a lame duck Prime Minister’ says former Labour adviser
item.description:
It’s been a tumultuous week for Britain’s Prime Minister, Sir Keir Starmer, who is facing a leadership crisis. Following a chaotic week in which nearly 90 Labour MPs called on Starmer to resign, along with a series of ministerial resignations, the Prime Minister is clinging on to power.
Former Health Secretary Wes Streeting has said he would stand in any leadership contest, while Andy Burnham, Mayor of Greater Manchester, is plotting a return to Parliament after confirming he will stand in the Makerfield by-election.
To discuss the unfolding events, France 24’s Gavin Lee is joined by Karl Hansen, former Labour adviser and political writer for Tribune Magazine.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Around 10 'new' victims come forward in France's Epstein investigation
item.description: French prosecutors investigating Jeffrey Epstein’s alleged trafficking network say around ten previously unidentified suspected victims have recently contacted authorities, widening a probe into possible abuse committed in France or involving French accomplices linked to the late financier.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: White House to host mass prayer event to promote Christian origins of the US
item.description: The White House is making prayer a focal point of this weekend’s celebrations marking the US's 250th anniversary, staging a large-scale mass prayer that underscores the Trump administration’s wider push to amplify Christianity’s role in public and political life.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 15,000 flowers a day: Behind the scenes at the Carlton Cannes
item.description: Using between 10,000 and 15,000 flowers a day to transform every space inside the Carlton Cannes: this is the world of floral designer Djordje Varda, who takes us behind the scenes of his extraordinary daily routine 💐
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 🔴 Ukraine launches around 600 drones at Russia, Zelensky claims ‘justified’ attack
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: LGBTI+ rights: May 17th, international day against homophobia
item.description: Flora Bolter, Co-director of the LGBTI+ Observatory at the Jean Jaurès Foundation in Paris, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak in DR Congo
item.description: General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus declared the Ebola disease outbreak in Congo and neighboring Uganda a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday after more than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths. In a post on X, the World Health Organization said the outbreak does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency like the COVID-19 pandemic, and advised against the closure of international borders.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Joint US-Nigeria operation killed a senior militant leader of IS
item.description: President Donald Trump said that a joint operation by U.S. and Nigerian forces killed a top leader of the Islamic State group in Nigeria.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Cannes: Palestinian paracycling crew formed by amputee athletes from Gaza arrive
item.description: Members of "Gaza Sunbirds", a Palestinian paracycling crew formed by amputee athletes from Gaza, made a symbolic arrival by sea at the Cannes Film Festival for the screening of a documentary about them.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Betting on war: Mysterious traders make millions on well-timed bets on Iran
item.description: Did traders have confidential information on the oil market? The US Congress has demanded answers.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Palestinian president’s son Yasser Abbas elected to top Fatah post
item.description: Yasser Abbas, the 64-year-old son of ageing Palestinian president Mahmoud Abbas, won a seat Sunday on the central committee of the Fatah party, initial results showed. The election extended the family's grip on the movement that has dominated the Palestinian Authority (PA) for decades. The PA is under mounting pressure to hold long-delayed presidential and parliamentary elections.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East: Israel attacks south Lebanon day after ceasefire extension
item.description: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK: Tens of thousands march in London in two separate protests
item.description: Tens of thousands of people marched through central London on Saturday in two separate protests - one against high levels of immigration and a perceived Islamic threat to British identity, and another in support of Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda
item.description: The World Health Organization on Sunday declared an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic ofCongo and Uganda a "public health emergency of international concern", posing risks to neighbouring countries after recording 88 deaths and more than 300 suspected cases in the region.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: $200,000 reward to catch Monica Witt wanted on espionage charges in Iran
item.description: The FBI is offering a $200,000 reward for information leading to capture and prosecution of a former U.S. Air Force counterintelligence specialist who defected to Iran in 2013 and was later charged with revealing classified information to the Tehran government. Monica Elfriede Witt, 47, was indicted by a federal grand jury in February 2019 on charges of espionage, including transmitting national defense information to the government of Iran.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
